medical_history.chief_complaint: slowly-progressive gait impairment and postural instability
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: two-year history of a slowly-progressive gait impairment and postural instability, which caused frequent falls
medical_history.past_medical_history: unremarkable, without endocrine disturbances, such as primary ovarian insufficiency syndrome or hypothyroidism
medical_history.family_history: Her 60-year-old son had similar symptoms since age 54 and the brain MRI revealed bilateral MCP sign and hyperintensity in the corpus callosum. Her son's FMR1 gene analysis revealed a premutation (100 CGG repeats), confirming the diagnosis of FXTAS. Patient's nephews had the classic presentation of fragile-X mental retardation syndrome.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Patient was unable to perform a tandem gait and had axial rigidity and prominent postural instability in neurologic examination. There were no other movement disorders, cognitive or psychiatric symptoms, and there was no supranuclear gaze palsy.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Hummingbird sign (midbrain atrophy with no pontine atrophy), reduced nigrossome-1 in posterior aspect of the substantia nigra suggesting impairment in nigroestriatal pathway

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Axial T2-sequenced imaging (Patient's son brain MRI)
radiographic_examinations.findings: Bilateral abnormal high signal intensity in the middle cerebellar peduncles (MCP sign) and hyperintensity in the corpus callosum
Mild mesencephalic atrophy resembling the hummingbird sign
Mesencephalic atrophy with interpeduncular fossa enlargement and postero-lateral concavity
Reduced nigrossome-1 in posterior aspect of substantia nigra probably related to nigro-estriatal pathway degeneration

other_tests.findings: Unable to perform a tandem gait, axial rigidity, and prominent postural instability
other_tests.changes_over_time: Symptoms remained stable over three years of follow-up
